title: [CI:DOCS] man pages: sort flags, and keep them that way
body:
Command flags (OPTIONS) in man pages have to date been in
haphazard order. Sometimes that order is sensible, e.g.,
most-important options first, but more often they're
just in arbitrary places. This makes life hard for users.

Here, I update the man-page-check Makefile script so it
checks and enforces alphabetical order in OPTIONS sections.
Then -- the hard part -- update all existing man pages to
conform to this requirement.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

My sincere apologies. I should've done this long ago.

While catching up on PR emails this morning, a scary comment caught my eye: something about "please put this option in its proper place". I was surprised because I thought that was already part of our checks.

Some of these changes are unfortunate -- I kind of like having `--uncompressed` right next to `--compress`, for instance, or grouping `--latest` near `--all` -- but on the whole I think users would benefit from sorted options.
First flake filed as #13626. Second flake looks like the `quay.io` outage we've been seeing the past two days.
